segments.text: Good evening from the Arnolfini in Bristol and welcome to the Sky News leaders debate.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Round two of the first ever televised prime ministerial debates in the UK.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Well, the three men who want to run the country after May 6th are here and raring to go.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: So please welcome David Cameron, Nick Clegg and Gordon Brown.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: The agreed theme for the first half of this debate is international affairs.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Each leader will make a short opening statement before taking questions from the audience.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: In the second half tonight, we'll move on to general issues.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Now, lots have been drawn to decide who goes first, and we begin with the current Prime Minister and leader of the Labour Party, Gordon Brown.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Mr Brown.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: This may have the feel of a TV popularity contest, but in truth,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: This is an election about Britain's future, a fight for your future and for your jobs.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: If it's all about style and PR, count me out.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: If it's about the big decisions, if it's about judgement, it's about delivering a better future for this country, I'm your man.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: Ahead are huge challenges, delivering the economic recovery in jobs,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: bringing our brave troops safely home from Afghanistan, keeping our streets free of terrorism, building alliances in Europe against nuclear weapons, against climate change, against poverty, and to deal with our banks.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: Now not everyone has the answers, but I say get the big decisions wrong and Britain's security and jobs are at risk.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: Get the big decisions right and we can have a prosperous, fairer, greener and better Britain.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: Like me or not, I can deliver that plan and the way to do it is with a majority Labour government.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_10

segments.text: Thank you, Mr Brown.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Next, the leader of the Conservative Party, David Cameron.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Thank you.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: It's clear from last week's debate that the country wants change.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: But the question is, what sort of change and whose best place to lead that change?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: If you vote Conservative, you will get a new team running the country from May the 7th.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: And you won't be stuck with what you've got now.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: But real change comes from your values.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: And there, there are big differences between us.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: I believe that we need to do more to help families.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: They are the absolute bedrock of a strong society.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: I want government to be accountable.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: I want less waste, less bureaucracy, stopping the jobs tax, but making sure we have good public services that you get good value for money for.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: As we're going to discuss tonight, I want us to keep our defences strong, I want to keep our borders secure and our country safe.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: but real change.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: Real change comes not just from politicians, but from when we all recognise that we have responsibilities.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: We're all in this together, and that's how we'll build the big and strong society I believe we need in our country.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04

segments.text: Thank you, Mr Cameron.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: And finally in this round, Nick Clegg, the leader of the Liberal Democrats.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: I am so proud of the values that have made our country so great.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: Democracy, human rights, the rule of law.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: But the sad truth is that in recent years our governments under the old parties have let those values down.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: We shouldn't have sent soldiers into battle without the right equipment.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: We shouldn't be facing allegations of complicity in torture.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: We shouldn't have invaded Iraq.

segments.text: I want us to lead in Europe, not complain from the sidelines.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: I want us to lead in creating a world free of nuclear weapons.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: And I want us to lead on the biggest challenge of all, climate change.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: Now my family knows what British values really mean.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: My mother was freed by British troops from a prisoner of war camp.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: And I think if we do things differently, we can be proud once again of the role we can play as a force for good in the world.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03

segments.text: Thank you, Mr Clegg.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: Now, those are tonight's opening statement.

segments.text: And there'll also be some questions in person from Sky News viewers who emailed us.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02

segments.text: I'm just asking for a simple, honest answer to a big question, which is that because of the chaos in the system in the past, we have lots of people who are here.

segments.text: I mean all I'd say is that the more they argue amongst each other, the more it's absolutely clear to me the only way you'll get real change, real action, sensible action on this issue is from a Conservative government.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04
